l1: Question 1 — When did the cluster issue first appear?
Question 2 — Is the problem affecting all nodes or a specific one?
Question 3 — Are clustered roles failing over unexpectedly?
Question 4 — Has the cluster configuration been modified recently?
Question 5 — Is the issue intermittent or persistent?
Question 6 — Are applications reporting downtime or just warnings?
l2: Advanced question 1 — Are there any recent OS patches or updates applied to nodes?
Advanced question 2 — Is the cluster quorum configuration healthy?
Advanced question 3 — Are cluster networks properly configured and redundant?
Advanced question 4 — Is shared storage accessible from all nodes?
Advanced question 5 — Are there performance drops before failovers?
Advanced question 6 — Is the issue isolated to a single cluster role or multiple?

l1: Command or question 1 — Open Failover Cluster Manager and check node status
Command or question 2 — Run PowerShell Get-ClusterNode to confirm health
Command or question 3 — Verify clustered roles with Get-ClusterGroup
Command or question 4 — Review Event Viewer for cluster-related warnings
Command or question 5 — Check cluster networks with Get-ClusterNetwork
l2: Advanced command 1 — Run Get-ClusterQuorum to verify quorum configuration
Advanced command 2 — Use Get-ClusterLog -UseLocalTime -Destination C:\ClusterLog to generate detailed logs
Advanced command 3 — Run Test-Cluster to validate configuration
Advanced command 4 — Query storage connectivity with Get-ClusterResource
Advanced command 5 — Review performance counters for cluster heartbeat latency
Advanced command 6 — Check cluster registry hive consistency across nodes

l1: Resolution step 1 — Restart the Cluster Service on affected nodes
Resolution step 2 — Verify network connectivity between nodes
Resolution step 3 — Ensure shared storage is online and accessible
Resolution step 4 — Rebalance clustered roles manually if needed
Resolution step 5 — Confirm quorum configuration matches design
l2: Advanced fix 1 — Run Test-Cluster and remediate reported issues
Advanced fix 2 — Update NIC drivers and firmware for cluster networks
Advanced fix 3 — Reconfigure quorum type (Node Majority, Disk Witness, File Share Witness)
Advanced fix 4 — Validate and repair cluster database consistency
Advanced fix 5 — Escalate to Microsoft support if cluster corruption suspected
Advanced fix 6 — Perform rolling upgrade or patching across nodes to stabilize

l1: Step 1 — Export Event Viewer logs from Applications and Services Logs → Microsoft → Windows → FailoverClustering → Operational
Step 2 — Run Get-ClusterNode and export results
Step 3 — Execute Get-ClusterGroup and save output
Step 4 — Capture screenshot of Failover Cluster Manager
Step 5 — Collect OS build and patch level
l2: Advanced collection 1 — Export Get-ClusterLog -UseLocalTime -Destination C:\ClusterLog detailed report
Advanced collection 2 — Collect Test-Cluster validation results
Advanced collection 3 — Gather performance monitor counters for cluster heartbeat
Advanced collection 4 — Export Get-ClusterQuorum configuration
Advanced collection 5 — Collect NIC firmware/driver versions for all nodes
Advanced collection 6 — Gather cluster registry hive dumps for consistency checks

symptom_slug.title: Cluster nodes unstable or roles failing over

steps.action: Run Get-ClusterNode to confirm node state
steps.expect: Node shows down or paused state
Errors reported in networking or storage

steps.action: Restart Cluster Service and repair quorum

steps.expect: All roles online and no new errors
